title: The Baldwin Effect: within-lifetime learning accelerating evolution
text: Baldwin Effect (Baldwin 1896; Simpson 1953) neo-Darwinian mechanism phenotypic plasticity — ability organism adjust behaviour within lifetime — can accelerate genetic evolution without Lamarckian inheritance. logic follows: novel environment, genetically fixed behaviours suboptimal. Individuals can learn (copy successful neighbours) survive long enough reproduce, even genome yet encode optimal behaviour. learners reproduce , genetic variants predispose faster deeper learning spread population. generations, benefit learning decreases genome gradually encodes -learned behaviour directly — process called genetic assimilation (Waddington 1942). clade provides two within-lifetime learning modules can used independently together study dynamics: Within-lifetime RL (rl_mode = "actor_critic"): REINFORCE baseline updates action-selection layer agent’s neural network tick, driven energy-gain reward. Social learning (social_learning = TRUE): prestige-biased copying — agent blends fraction highest-energy neighbour’s output-layer weights policy, following strategy Henrich & Gil-White (2001).

title: The Baldwin Effect: within-lifetime learning accelerating evolution
text: experiment compares three conditions 300 ticks 30×30 grid:
code: library(clade) library(ggplot2) library(patchwork) base <- default_specs() base$grid_rows     <- 30L base$grid_cols     <- 30L base$n_agents_init <- 80L base$max_agents    <- 500L base$max_ticks     <- 300L base$random_seed   <- 42L # Start in a low-resource environment to create selection pressure for learning base$grass_rate    <- 0.03 base$grass_init_prob <- 0.3 # Condition 1: no within-lifetime learning s_base <- base s_base$rl_mode        <- "none" s_base$social_learning <- FALSE env_base <- run_alife(s_base)  # Condition 2: individual RL only s_rl <- base s_rl$rl_mode        <- "actor_critic" s_rl$rl_update_freq <- 1L s_rl$social_learning <- FALSE env_rl <- run_alife(s_rl)  # Condition 3: RL + prestige-biased social learning (full Baldwin pathway) s_both <- base s_both$rl_mode             <- "actor_critic" s_both$rl_update_freq      <- 1L s_both$social_learning      <- TRUE s_both$social_learning_freq <- 5L s_both$social_learning_rate <- 0.1 env_both <- run_alife(s_both)

title: The Baldwin Effect: within-lifetime learning accelerating evolution
text: RL + SL condition typically reaches higher mean energy sooner either baseline RL-condition, reflecting two-step Baldwin pathway: individual reinforcement discovers locally good policies, social copying spreads successful policies faster genetic transmission alone.
code: d_base <- get_run_data(env_base)$ticks d_rl   <- get_run_data(env_rl)$ticks d_both <- get_run_data(env_both)$ticks  # Combine into a single tidy data frame d <- rbind(   transform(d_base, condition = "Baseline"),   transform(d_rl,   condition = "RL only"),   transform(d_both, condition = "RL + SL") ) d$condition <- factor(d$condition, levels = c("Baseline", "RL only", "RL + SL")) p_agents <- ggplot(d, aes(t, n_agents, colour = condition)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   labs(x = "Tick", y = "Population size",        colour = NULL, title = "Population size") +   theme_minimal(base_size = 12)  p_energy <- ggplot(d, aes(t, mean_energy, colour = condition)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   labs(x = "Tick", y = "Mean energy",        colour = NULL, title = "Mean energy per agent") +   theme_minimal(base_size = 12)  p_agents / p_energy + plot_layout(guides = "collect") &   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

text: Genetic assimilation leaves signature population’s genetic diversity: early run, diversity maintained genome yet encode optimal policy; later, learned behaviour becomes genetically fixed, diversity decreases small number well-adapted genotypes dominate. steeper late-run decline RL + SL condition relative baseline consistent accelerated assimilation: learning buffer reduces selection variance early, allowing genetic variants persist, benefit learning narrows genome catches , driving sharper selective sweep.
code: ggplot(d, aes(t, genetic_diversity, colour = condition)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   labs(x = "Tick", y = "Mean pairwise genome distance",        colour = NULL,        title = "Genetic diversity — signature of genetic assimilation") +   theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

title: The Baldwin Effect: within-lifetime learning accelerating evolution
text: epigenetics = TRUE module adds second non-genetic inheritance channel: methylation marks accumulated agent’s lifetime transmitted offspring probability epigenetic_inheritance. can accelerate Baldwin pathway offspring inherits parent’s genome also prior synaptic weights canalize. mean_prior_sigma (mean standard deviation BNN weight distributions) measures certain agents action-selection policy. Lower values indicate canalized — potentially heritably fixed — neural circuits. epigenetic condition show faster sigma reduction offspring inherit methylation marks bias sigma contraction towards parent’s effective policy.
code: s_epi <- s_both s_epi$epigenetics            <- TRUE s_epi$epigenetic_inheritance <- 0.5 s_epi$methylation_rate       <- 0.2 s_epi$demethylation_rate     <- 0.05 env_epi <- run_alife(s_epi) d_epi <- get_run_data(env_epi)$ticks d2 <- rbind(   transform(d_both, condition = "RL + SL"),   transform(d_epi,  condition = "RL + SL + Epigenetics") ) ggplot(d2, aes(t, mean_prior_sigma, colour = condition)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   labs(x = "Tick", y = "Mean BNN prior sigma",        colour = NULL,        title = "BNN uncertainty — canalization via epigenetics") +   theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

title: Custom Modules: Extending clade with Per-Tick Hooks
text: hook function receives snapshot — list structure env object returned run_alife():
code: register_module(fn, when = "post_tick", name = NULL) list_modules() clear_modules()

title: Custom Modules: Extending clade with Per-Tick Hooks
text: Record number agents top energy quartile every tick. <<- super-assignment operator reaches calling environment top_quartile_counts updated outside hook closure.
code: library(clade)  top_quartile_counts <- integer(0)  register_module(   function(snap) {     energies <- vapply(snap$agents, function(ag) ag$energy, numeric(1L))     q75      <- quantile(energies, 0.75)     # <<- writes into the parent environment (outside the hook)     top_quartile_counts <<- c(top_quartile_counts, sum(energies >= q75))     snap   # always return snap unchanged (or modified)   },   when = "post_tick",   name = "top_quartile_tracker" )  s <- default_specs() s$max_ticks <- 100L env <- run_alife(s) clear_modules()   # always clear after the run  cat(sprintf("Mean top-quartile size: %.1f over %d ticks\n",             mean(top_quartile_counts), length(top_quartile_counts)))  plot(top_quartile_counts, type = "l",      xlab = "Tick", ylab = "Agents in top energy quartile")

title: Custom Modules: Extending clade with Per-Tick Hooks
text: Record spatial variance agent positions proxy population dispersal tick.
code: spread_log <- numeric(0)  register_module(   function(snap) {     if (length(snap$agents) == 0L) {       spread_log <<- c(spread_log, NA_real_)       return(snap)     }     xs <- vapply(snap$agents, function(ag) ag$x, numeric(1L))     ys <- vapply(snap$agents, function(ag) ag$y, numeric(1L))     # Toroidal variance: use circular formula for modular coordinates     spread <- var(xs) + var(ys)     spread_log <<- c(spread_log, spread)     snap   },   when = "post_tick",   name = "spatial_spread" )  s <- default_specs() s$dispersal_evolution <- TRUE s$max_ticks           <- 200L env <- run_alife(s) clear_modules()  plot(spread_log, type = "l", xlab = "Tick",      ylab = "Spatial variance (x + y)",      main = "Population spatial spread over time")

title: Custom Modules: Extending clade with Per-Tick Hooks
text: Add 10 units energy agents tick 100, simulating sudden resource pulse.
code: register_module(   function(snap) {     if (snap$t == 100L) {       snap$agents <- lapply(snap$agents, function(ag) {         ag$energy <- ag$energy + 10.0         ag       })       message("[module] Energy pulse applied at tick ", snap$t)     }     snap   },   when = "post_tick",   name = "energy_pulse_t100" )  s <- default_specs() s$max_ticks <- 200L env <- run_alife(s) clear_modules()  # The energy spike should be visible in env$progress at tick 100 data <- get_run_data(env) plot(data$ticks$t, data$ticks$mean_energy, type = "l",      xlab = "Tick", ylab = "Mean energy",      main = "Energy pulse at tick 100") abline(v = 100, col = "red", lty = 2)

title: Custom Modules: Extending clade with Per-Tick Hooks
text: Multiple modules can registered run registration order:
code: counts_a <- integer(0) counts_b <- numeric(0)  register_module(   function(snap) {     counts_a <<- c(counts_a, length(snap$agents))     snap   },   name = "pop_counter" )  register_module(   function(snap) {     energies <- vapply(snap$agents, function(ag) ag$energy, numeric(1L))     counts_b <<- c(counts_b, mean(energies))     snap   },   name = "energy_tracker" )  cat("Registered modules:\n") print(list_modules())   # shows name and when for each module  s <- default_specs() s$max_ticks <- 50L env <- run_alife(s) clear_modules()

title: Custom Modules: Extending clade with Per-Tick Hooks
text: Always call clear_modules() run. Registered modules persist across calls run_alife() within R session. Forgetting clear causes modules accumulate run subsequent simulations unexpectedly. Keep hooks fast. Modules run every tick. hook slow (e.g. writes disk, complex computation), can dominate total runtime. Profile short run using long simulations. Use <<- accumulation. hooks closures, use <<- (assign(..., envir = parent.env())) write results container defined outside hook. Return snap unmodified want observe. hook must always return snap. logging, return unchanged. modify snap$agents, changes propagate subsequent modules simulation state. Module execution order. Modules run order registered. module B depends state set module , register first.
code: # Safe pattern: register, run, clear register_module(my_hook, name = "my_hook") env <- run_alife(specs) clear_modules()   # <- never skip this

title: Genetic Diversity Search in clade
text: Genetic diversity quantifies breadth heritable variation simulated population. proxy evolutionary potential (genetically uniform population little raw material selection act ), resilience environmental change (diverse populations less likely uniformly maladapted novel challenge), richness phenotypic exploration (high diversity increases probability least one lineage colonise new niche). conservation biology, loss genetic diversity leading indicator extinction risk. artificial life research, diversity substrate open-ended evolution depends. Finding parameter combinations sustain high diversity — understanding trade-offs entail — therefore central design problem fields. clade provides four complementary tools search: workflow moves coarse fine: screen search_random(), characterise trade-offs search_map_elites(), maximise search_cmaes().

title: Genetic Diversity Search in clade
text: search_random() draws n_samples independent parameter configurations supplied ranges evaluates . fast enough run interactively right entry point prior knowledge regions parameter space promising. example screens three parameters simultaneously. sample draws mutation_sd, grass_rate, n_agents_init independently uniformly specified ranges, runs full simulation, records mean genetic diversity. result data frame sorted descending score. row corresponds one simulation; score column holds mean genetic diversity across ticks. highest-diversity configurations found random sampling serve starting points targeted searches . specs_list attribute stores full specs row, ordered match data frame. Retrieve best configuration :
code: library(clade)  result <- search_random(   specs_base    = default_specs(),   search_params = list(     mutation_sd   = c(0.01, 0.5),     grass_rate    = c(0.05, 0.8),     n_agents_init = c(20L, 150L)   ),   n_samples  = 30L,   objective  = "genetic_diversity" )  # Top configurations by genetic diversity head(result)  # Partial-dependence plot for mutation_sd plot(result$mutation_sd, result$score,      xlab = "mutation_sd", ylab = "mean genetic diversity",      pch  = 19, col = "steelblue") #>   mutation_sd grass_rate n_agents_init score #> 1       0.312      0.621           127 0.847 #> 2       0.287      0.583           119 0.831 #> 3       0.341      0.548           143 0.819 #> 4       0.198      0.701            98 0.807 #> 5       0.263      0.612           134 0.793 #> 6       0.415      0.469           110 0.778 best_specs <- attr(result, "specs_list")[[1]] cat("Best mutation_sd:", best_specs$mutation_sd, "\n") cat("Best grass_rate:", best_specs$grass_rate, "\n") #> Best mutation_sd: 0.312 #> Best grass_rate: 0.621

title: Genetic Diversity Search in clade
text: MAP-Elites algorithm (Mouret & Clune 2015) fills grid niches behavioural space. Unlike standard optimiser, converge single point. Instead, every cell archive holds parameter set achieves particular (diversity, population) combination highest diversity score. Empty cells indicate combinations simulated configuration achieved. illumination achievable space key advantage random search: can read just “maximises diversity” “diversity achievable population size” — biologically meaningful trade-. Interpreting heatmap: Filled cells show achievable (diversity, population) pairs. colour encodes objective score: brighter means higher diversity within niche. Empty cells (shown grey) combinations parameter set produced. high-diversity, large-population cells empty, combination may intrinsically unattainable chosen model. archive (result$archive) list length equal number cells. Non-null elements contain $specs, $score, $behavioural_descriptor.
code: result <- search_map_elites(   specs_base   = default_specs(),   archive_dims = list(     genetic_diversity = seq(0, 1, by = 0.1),     n_agents          = seq(0, 200, by = 20)   ),   n_iterations = 300L,   objective    = "genetic_diversity",   verbose      = FALSE )  # Archive heatmap: x = genetic_diversity niche, y = n_agents niche, # fill = objective score (NA = empty cell) result$map # Extract all non-empty cells and compare their mutation_sd non_empty <- Filter(Negate(is.null), result$archive) cat("Filled cells:", length(non_empty), "\n") cat("mutation_sd range in archive:",     range(sapply(non_empty, function(x) x$specs$mutation_sd)), "\n") #> Filled cells: 47 #> mutation_sd range in archive: 0.0312 0.4871

title: Genetic Diversity Search in clade
text: search_cmaes() implements pure-R CMA-ES (Hansen 2006) — external packages required. finds single parameter set maximises objective. Use want one best answer rather map trade-offs. use CMA-ES vs MAP-Elites: Use search_cmaes() want highest possible diversity care population-size cost. converges single point. Use search_map_elites() want understand diversity scales outcomes (population size, speciation, cooperation level). reveals full Pareto-like frontier achievable outcomes. CMA-ES faster per run single-objective answer; MAP-Elites informative per compute budget multi-objective insight.
code: result <- search_cmaes(   specs_base = default_specs(),   params     = c("mutation_sd", "grass_rate", "crossover_rate"),   objective  = "genetic_diversity",   n_iterations = 20L,   popsize    = 10L )  cat("Best diversity:", result$score, "\n") cat("Best params:\n") print(result$specs[c("mutation_sd", "grass_rate", "crossover_rate")]) #> Best diversity: 0.863 #> Best params: #>   mutation_sd grass_rate crossover_rate #>         0.301      0.621          0.142

title: Genetic Diversity Search in clade
text: four search functions accept custom objective function place column name. function receives env object returned run_alife() must return numeric scalar. example defines objective rewards diversity sustained population size. Runs go extinct (mean population 10) receive score zero regardless transient diversity, preventing optimiser finding parameters briefly achieve high diversity collapse. custom_obj function can passed search_map_elites(), search_cmaes(), search_gradient() without modification.
code: custom_obj <- function(env) {   d   <- get_run_data(env)$ticks   div <- mean(d$genetic_diversity, na.rm = TRUE)   pop <- mean(d$n_agents,          na.rm = TRUE)   if (pop < 10) return(0)      # discard runs that go near-extinct   div * log1p(pop / 50)        # reward diversity weighted by log-population }  result <- search_random(   specs_base    = default_specs(),   search_params = list(     mutation_sd = c(0.01, 0.4),     grass_rate  = c(0.1,  0.7)   ),   n_samples = 20L,   objective = custom_obj )  head(result)

title: Genetic Diversity Search in clade
text: Zygote.jl computes exact gradients tracing Julia code. requires every operation forward pass differentiable. run_alife() contains discrete events differentiable: Agent death (Bernoulli draw — discontinuous energy) Agent birth (conditional energy >= min_repro_energy) argmax brain outputs (non-differentiable everywhere) Stochastic mating (Categorical sample) Autodiff operations either fail trace time silently produce zero gradient. correct optimisers class stochastic discrete-event simulation :
code: # CMA-ES does not require differentiability. # It treats run_alife() as a black box and estimates curvature from # population statistics alone. result <- search_cmaes(   default_specs(),   params       = c("grass_rate", "mutation_sd"),   objective    = "genetic_diversity",   n_iterations = 50L )

what: 7. Finding viable parameters for the complex landscape
title: Genetic Diversity Search in clade
text: parameter combinations allow organisms survive. running CMA-ES worth mapping viable region — set landscape densities energy values population go extinct. search_viability() single call. viable region identified, run CMA-ES inside using scenario-specific objective: tune_complex_landscape() pre-configures five landscape parameters (shrub_density, canopy_density, shrub_energy, canopy_energy, shrub_growth_rate) uses objective_complex_landscape(), measures joint increase wing size, niche diversity, survival.
code: library(clade)  # Step 1: identify which shrub/canopy density combinations are viable s <- default_specs() s$complex_landscape <- TRUE  vmap <- search_viability(   s,   param_x = "shrub_density",  values_x = seq(0.1, 0.6, by = 0.1),   param_y = "canopy_density", values_y = seq(0.05, 0.35, by = 0.1),   n_reps  = 3L )  vmap$map     # ggplot2 heatmap: green = viable, red = extinct vmap$data    # data frame with viability, mean_final_pop per cell # Step 2: CMA-ES within the viable region (set sensible starting values from # Step 1 first, or let tune_complex_landscape() handle defaults) tuned <- tune_complex_landscape(default_specs(), n_iterations = 80L) tuned$specs          # optimal parameter set tuned$history$sigma  # step-size decay — smaller sigma = converged

title: Genetic Diversity Search in clade
text: workflow applies IFfolk module. First map transfer rates parliament costs allow helper tendency evolve : optimise tune_iffolk(): objective_iffolk() fits linear regression mean_helper_tendency time rewards upward trends (slope x 1000) plus per-agent transfer bonus. much sensitive simply maximising mean helper tendency final tick.
code: s <- default_specs() s$iffolk_selection    <- TRUE s$cooperative_breeding <- TRUE  vmap_iff <- search_viability(   s,   param_x  = "iffolk_transfer",   values_x = seq(1, 6, by = 1),   param_y  = "parliament_cost",   values_y = seq(0.1, 0.8, by = 0.2),   n_reps   = 3L,   objective = "mean_helper_tendency"  # also report evolutionary outcome )  vmap_iff$map   # heatmap coloured by viability tuned_iff <- tune_iffolk(default_specs(), n_iterations = 80L) tuned_iff$specs$iffolk_transfer   # optimal transfer amount

text: clade crosses R–Julia boundary exactly per run_alife() call, regardless number ticks number agents. entire tick loop, sensing, neural network forward pass, action selection, movement, eating, reproduction, death runs inside single Julia process. 200 agents 1,000 ticks roughly 25× faster Rcpp-based alifeR backend, scales smoothly populations ~100,000 agents workstation. first call R session pays one-start-cost (~30–60 s) Julia compilation. text: models. three-layer habitat: ground (grass), shrubs (mid-level), canopy (top). Shrubs canopy higher energy density slower regrowth. Canopy access requires minimum wing_size (heritable trait). creates selection pressure wing-size evolution niche partitioning. scenario structurally analogous cephalopod paradox (Liedtke & Fromhage 2019): rich resource accessible agents evolved morphology exploit . Key parameters. Expected output. mean_wing_size rises time. n_canopy_agents increases wing-size evolution crosses canopy_threshold. Niche entropy (ground/shrub/canopy mix) first rises stabilises. Use tune_complex_landscape() find parameter combinations produce strong wing-size evolution CMA-ES: Expected output: agents stratify across ground, shrub, canopy layers wing size evolves past canopy threshold.
code: s <- default_specs() s$complex_landscape   <- TRUE s$shrub_density       <- 0.35 s$canopy_density      <- 0.15 s$canopy_energy       <- 55.0 s$canopy_threshold    <- 0.6 s$wing_size_init_mean <- 0.1 s$max_ticks           <- 400L  env  <- run_alife(s) data <- get_run_data(env)  # Wing size evolution plot(data$ticks$t, data$ticks$mean_wing_size, type = "l",      xlab = "Tick", ylab = "Mean wing size",      main = "Wing size evolution in the forest world") tuned <- tune_complex_landscape(default_specs(), n_iterations = 80L) tuned$specs   # optimal landscape parameters

title: Biological Scenarios and Modules
text: models. Predator-prey coevolution one oldest empirically grounded problems population biology. classical Lotka-Volterra equations predict sustained oscillations predator abundance tracks prey abundance characteristic lag (Lotka 1925; Volterra 1928). clade, predators autonomous agents energy budgets, movement rules, reproduction thresholds, oscillations emerge individual behaviour rather imposed differential equations. Key parameters. Expected output. Watch n_agents (prey) n_predators time. Track mean_prior_sigma data$ticks. column decline approximately 0.3 (broad, flexible priors) toward 0.05 (narrow, assimilated priors) selection repeatedly rewards weight configurations. decline rate accelerate genetic diversity high, large pool variants provides stronger selection gradient. Expected output: mean prior sigma declines monotonically broad (flexible, ~0.3) narrow (assimilated, ~0.05), steepest drop periods high genetic diversity. fitted linear trend confirms directional pattern.
code: library(clade) library(ggplot2)  s <- default_specs() s$brain_type <- "bnn" s$max_ticks  <- 500L  env  <- run_alife(s) data <- get_run_data(env)  ggplot(data$ticks, aes(x = t, y = mean_prior_sigma)) +   geom_line(colour = "#2166ac", linewidth = 0.8, alpha = 0.7) +   geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE,               colour = "#d6604d", fill = "#f4a582", alpha = 0.4) +   labs(     title    = "Genetic assimilation of learned behaviour (Baldwin Effect)",     subtitle = "BNN prior sigma declines as behaviour canalizes into the genome",     x = "Tick", y = expression("Mean prior " * sigma)   ) +   theme_minimal()

what: The cephalopod paradox — short lifespan selects for fast learning
title: Biological Scenarios and Modules
text: models. Classical life-history theory predicts long-lived organisms invest heavily learning, long lifespan provides enough time recoup energetic costs large brain (payback period argument). cephalopod paradox inverts prediction: octopuses squid among shortest lifespans large-brained animal, yet express sophisticated within-lifetime learning. Liedtke & Fromhage (2019) formalised resolution: lifespan brief genetic evolution converge food-finding solutions, within-lifetime learning becomes viable mechanism adaptive behaviour. , complex_landscape = TRUE creates heterogeneous resource topology solved fixed behavioural rule, learning_rate_evolution = TRUE allows genome optimise quickly agents update neural weights experience. Key parameters. Expected output. evolved mean learning rate (recorded final tick run) highest short max_age values decline lifespan increases, long-lived agents can afford rely genetic evolution encode solutions directly. relationship need monotone short lifespans, agents little experience benefit learning. Expected output: evolved learning rate highest shortest lifespan (max_age = 20) declines lifespan increases, consistent cephalopod paradox. Genetic evolution substitutes within-lifetime learning payback period long enough.
code: library(clade) library(ggplot2)  lifespans  <- c(20L, 50L, 100L, 200L) specs_list <- lapply(lifespans, function(ma) {   s <- default_specs()   s$max_age               <- ma   s$complex_landscape     <- TRUE   s$learning_rate_evolution <- TRUE   s$max_ticks             <- 400L   s }) names(specs_list) <- paste0("max_age_", lifespans)  results <- batch_alife(specs_list, n_cores = 4L)  lr_df <- do.call(rbind, mapply(function(env, ma) {   d <- get_run_data(env)$ticks   data.frame(     max_age       = ma,     mean_lr_final = tail(d$mean_learning_rate, 1L)   ) }, results, lifespans, SIMPLIFY = FALSE))  ggplot(lr_df, aes(x = max_age, y = mean_lr_final)) +   geom_line(colour = "#e08214", linewidth = 1) +   geom_point(size = 3, colour = "#8073ac") +   labs(     title    = "The cephalopod paradox: short lifespan selects for fast learning",     subtitle = "Evolved mean learning rate at end of run, by maximum lifespan",     x = "Maximum lifespan (ticks)", y = "Evolved mean learning rate"   ) +   theme_minimal()

code: library(clade) library(ggplot2)  make_s <- function(hypermut) {   s <- default_specs()   s$stress_hypermutation       <- hypermut   s$stress_threshold           <- 20.0   s$stress_mutation_multiplier <- 5.0   s$grass_rate                 <- 0.05   s$max_ticks                  <- 500L   s }  results <- batch_alife(   list(baseline = make_s(FALSE), hypermutation = make_s(TRUE)),   n_cores = 2L )  plot_df <- do.call(rbind, mapply(function(env, nm) {   d <- get_run_data(env)$ticks   data.frame(t = d$t, genetic_diversity = d$genetic_diversity, condition = nm) }, results, names(results), SIMPLIFY = FALSE))  ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = t, y = genetic_diversity, colour = condition)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.7, alpha = 0.8) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c(baseline = "#878787",                                  hypermutation = "#d6604d")) +   labs(     title    = "Stress hypermutation increases genetic diversity during resource crashes",     subtitle = "grass_rate = 0.05; stress_mutation_multiplier = 5",     x = "Tick", y = "Genetic diversity", colour = "Condition"   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Biological Scenarios and Modules
text: models. central quantity quantitative genetics narrow-sense heritability, h² — proportion phenotypic variance trait attributable additive genetic effects (Falconer & Mackay 1996; Lynch & Walsh 1998). trait respond natural selection unless h² > 0. estimate_heritability() function performs parent-offspring regression pedigree records accumulated simulation: offspring trait values regressed mid-parent values, slope estimates h². body_size_evolution = TRUE, body size polygenic trait encoded neural genome subject mutation selection, making suitable target heritability analysis within single run. Key parameters. Expected output. h² body_size substantially positive (0.3–0.7) early run, reflecting strong additive genetic architecture freshly initialised population. directional selection erodes genetic variance, h² may decline later ticks. 95% confidence interval regression exclude zero. h² energy expected lower, energy heavily influenced local environment. Expected output: parent-offspring regression body size positive slope (h² = 0.3-0.7). point one offspring plotted mid-parent value. shaded band 95% confidence interval regression line.
code: library(clade)  s <- default_specs() s$body_size_evolution <- TRUE s$max_ticks           <- 500L  env  <- run_alife(s) data <- get_run_data(env)  h2 <- estimate_heritability(data, trait = "body_size")  cat("Heritability h\u00b2:", round(h2$h2, 3), "\n") cat("95% CI: [", round(h2$ci[1], 3), ",", round(h2$ci[2], 3), "]\n")  plot(h2)  # parent-offspring regression scatter with fitted line

code: library(clade) library(ggplot2) library(patchwork)  make_s <- function(n_pred) {   s <- default_specs()   s$n_agents_init    <- 80L   s$n_predators_init <- n_pred   s$max_ticks        <- 500L   s }  results <- batch_alife(   list(no_predators = make_s(0L), predators = make_s(10L)),   n_cores = 2L )  plot_df <- do.call(rbind, mapply(function(env, nm) {   d <- get_run_data(env)$ticks   data.frame(     t                = d$t,     genetic_diversity = d$genetic_diversity,     mean_energy      = d$mean_energy,     condition        = nm   ) }, results, names(results), SIMPLIFY = FALSE))  p1 <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = t, y = genetic_diversity, colour = condition)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c(no_predators = "#4dac26",                                  predators    = "#d01c8b")) +   labs(title = "Predation and neural genome diversity",        x = "Tick", y = "Genetic diversity", colour = NULL) +   theme_minimal()  p2 <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = t, y = mean_energy, colour = condition)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c(no_predators = "#4dac26",                                  predators    = "#d01c8b")) +   labs(x = "Tick", y = "Mean energy", colour = NULL) +   theme_minimal()  p1 / p2

title: Biological Scenarios and Modules
text: models. Biological systems shaped multiple interacting selective pressures, individual contributions difficult disentangle nature. scenario applies one-module---time factorial design: condition activates exactly one module shared baseline, three summary statistics — mean genetic diversity, mean population size, mean energy — recorded across full run. Comparing modules isolation combining allows module’s unique contribution identified; -condition reveals emergent interactions predicted single-module results alone. Key parameters. Expected output. Modules imposing strong directional selection — predators disease — tend increase genetic diversity maintaining selective gradient; modules smooth energy acquisition — niche construction, social learning — may reduce within-population variance. -condition typically reveals non-additive interactions: cooperation kin selection can partially compensate disease mortality, niche construction can buffer energetic cost predation evasion. Expected output: horizontal bar chart mean genetic diversity across 14 conditions. Conditions imposing strong directional selection (predators, disease, mimicry) appear near top; -condition reveals emergent multi-module interactions.
code: library(clade) library(ggplot2)  make_specs <- function(...) {   s <- default_specs()   s$max_ticks <- 300L   args <- list(...)   for (nm in names(args)) s[[nm]] <- args[[nm]]   s }  specs_list <- list(   baseline    = make_specs(),   predators   = make_specs(n_predators_init = 5L),   disease     = make_specs(disease = TRUE),   kin         = make_specs(kin_selection = TRUE),   niche       = make_specs(niche_construction = TRUE),   rl          = make_specs(rl_mode = "actor_critic"),   social      = make_specs(social_learning = TRUE),   body_size   = make_specs(body_size_evolution = TRUE),   dispersal   = make_specs(dispersal_evolution = TRUE),   care        = make_specs(parental_care = TRUE),   mimicry     = make_specs(mimicry = TRUE, n_predators_init = 5L),   landscape   = make_specs(complex_landscape = TRUE),   cooperation = make_specs(cooperation_evolution = TRUE),   all_on      = make_specs(     n_predators_init      = 5L,     disease               = TRUE,     kin_selection         = TRUE,     niche_construction    = TRUE,     rl_mode               = "actor_critic",     social_learning       = TRUE,     body_size_evolution   = TRUE,     dispersal_evolution   = TRUE,     cooperation_evolution = TRUE   ) )  results <- batch_alife(specs_list, n_cores = 4L)  summary_df <- do.call(rbind, mapply(function(env, nm) {   d <- get_run_data(env)$ticks   data.frame(     condition      = nm,     mean_diversity = mean(d$genetic_diversity, na.rm = TRUE),     mean_pop       = mean(d$n_agents,          na.rm = TRUE),     mean_energy    = mean(d$mean_energy,       na.rm = TRUE)   ) }, results, names(results), SIMPLIFY = FALSE))  ggplot(summary_df,        aes(x = reorder(condition, mean_diversity), y = mean_diversity)) +   geom_col(fill = "#4dac26") +   coord_flip() +   labs(     title = "Mean genetic diversity by module condition",     x     = NULL,     y     = "Mean genetic diversity"   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Biological Scenarios and Modules
text: models. preceding sections isolate individual mechanisms establish clear cause--effect relationships. Real ecosystems combine many selective pressures simultaneously, interactions often non-additive. multi-module run demonstrates clade’s design genuinely compositional: combination modules can enabled together without special-casing, emergent dynamics richer sum parts. Key parameters. Expected output. single output metric tells full story. Look disease waves n_infected, predator oscillations n_predators, shelter accumulation n_shelters. interaction modules emergent: shelters reduce predation, alters population structure kin selection disease act. Expected output: prey (blue), predator (red), infected-agent (orange) time series show interacting waves. Disease suppresses prey density, turn reduces predator abundance, niche construction social learning modulate recovery rates.
code: library(clade) library(ggplot2) library(tidyr)  s <- default_specs() s$complex_landscape      <- TRUE s$grid_rows              <- 40L s$grid_cols              <- 40L s$n_agents_init          <- 120L s$n_predators_init       <- 5L s$disease                <- TRUE s$kin_selection          <- TRUE s$social_learning        <- TRUE s$rl_mode                <- "actor_critic" s$niche_construction     <- TRUE s$max_ticks              <- 500L  env  <- run_alife(s) tks  <- get_run_data(env)$ticks  dat_long <- pivot_longer(   tks[, c("t", "n_agents", "n_predators", "n_infected")],   cols      = c("n_agents", "n_predators", "n_infected"),   names_to  = "variable",   values_to = "value" ) dat_long$variable <- factor(   dat_long$variable,   levels = c("n_agents", "n_predators", "n_infected"),   labels = c("Prey agents", "Predators", "Infected agents") )  ggplot(dat_long, aes(x = t, y = value, colour = variable)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.7, alpha = 0.9) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c("Prey agents"     = "#1565C0",                "Predators"       = "#B71C1C",                "Infected agents" = "#F57F17"),     name = NULL   ) +   labs(     x     = "Tick",     y     = "Count",     title = "Kitchen-sink run: predation, disease, kin selection, RL, and niche construction"   ) +   theme_classic(base_size = 12)

title: Simulating evolution: a showcase of clade
text: environment rectangular grid. cell holds quantity grass — renewable resource representing primary productivity. Agents occupy individual cells, sense immediate neighbourhood, move according output neural brain. Energy earned grass minus energy spent maintenance determines survival reproduction. Final grid state 500 ticks (40 × 40, seed 42). Bright cells abundant grass; dark cells depleted. Agents (white dots, sized energy) cluster near high-productivity patches. Six-panel run summary: population size, mean energy (±SD ribbon), genetic diversity, births deaths per tick, grass coverage, BNN prior sigma (Baldwin Effect panel). plot_run() reveals characteristic logistic growth curve: population grows resource competition limits expansion, stabilises near carrying capacity set max_agents. BNN prior sigma panel tracks Baldwin Effect: agents’ genomes encode foraging solution, Bayesian prior narrows.
code: base <- default_specs() base$grid_rows     <- 40L base$grid_cols     <- 40L base$n_agents_init <- 30L base$max_ticks     <- 500L base$random_seed   <- 42L  env  <- run_alife(base, verbose = FALSE) data <- get_run_data(env) plot_environment(env) plot_run(data)

what: 3. Food availability as a selection pressure
title: Simulating evolution: a showcase of clade
text: compare three grass_rate conditions: abundant (0.5), default (0.1), scarce (0.02). Population size (left) mean age (right) across three food-availability conditions. Scarce food imposes stronger selection (older mean age) sustains fewer individuals. Scarce food (grass_rate = 0.02) sustains small highly adapted population; abundant food (grass_rate = 0.5) allows rapid growth reduces selective pressure. package default (grass_rate = 0.1) balances viability meaningful selection.
code: run_grass <- function(rate) {   s <- default_specs()   s$grid_rows     <- 20L; s$grid_cols     <- 20L   s$n_agents_init <- 20L; s$max_ticks     <- 300L   s$max_agents    <- 400L; s$grass_rate   <- rate   s$random_seed   <- 1L   cbind(get_run_data(run_alife(s, verbose = FALSE))$ticks,         grass_rate = rate) }  grass_results <- rbind(   run_grass(0.5), run_grass(0.1), run_grass(0.02) ) grass_results$condition <- factor(   paste0("grass_rate = ", grass_results$grass_rate),   levels = c("grass_rate = 0.5", "grass_rate = 0.1", "grass_rate = 0.02") )  p1 <- ggplot(grass_results, aes(x = t, y = n_agents, colour = condition)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("#1b7837", "#4dac26", "#d6604d")) +   labs(title = "Population size", x = "Tick", y = "N agents",        colour = NULL) +   theme_minimal() + theme(legend.position = "bottom")  p2 <- ggplot(grass_results, aes(x = t, y = mean_age, colour = condition)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("#1b7837", "#4dac26", "#d6604d")) +   labs(title = "Mean agent age", x = "Tick", y = "Age (ticks)",        colour = NULL) +   theme_minimal() + theme(legend.position = "bottom")  p1 | p2

title: Simulating evolution: a showcase of clade

code: library(clade) library(ggplot2)  ages   <- c(20L, 40L, 80L, 160L, 300L) n_reps <- 3L   # replicate each lifespan for robustness  results <- lapply(ages, function(ma) {   reps <- lapply(seq_len(n_reps), function(rep) {     s <- default_specs()     s$grid_rows               <- 20L     s$grid_cols               <- 20L     s$n_agents_init           <- 60L     s$max_agents              <- 300L     s$max_ticks               <- 400L     s$max_age                 <- ma     s$complex_landscape       <- TRUE     s$shrub_density           <- 0.4     s$shrub_energy            <- 30.0     s$shrub_growth_rate       <- 0.05     s$learning_rate_evolution <- TRUE     s$learning_rate_init_mean <- 0.05     s$learning_rate_min       <- 0.001     s$learning_rate_max       <- 0.5     s$random_seed             <- as.integer(rep * 100L + ma)     env  <- run_alife(s, verbose = FALSE)     data <- get_run_data(env)     tail_lr <- tail(data$ticks$mean_learning_rate[data$ticks$n_agents > 0], 50)     data.frame(max_age = ma, rep = rep, mean_learning = mean(tail_lr))   })   do.call(rbind, reps) })  df <- do.call(rbind, results)  # Aggregate over replicates agg <- aggregate(mean_learning ~ max_age, data = df, FUN = mean) agg$sd <- aggregate(mean_learning ~ max_age, data = df, FUN = sd)$mean_learning  ggplot(agg, aes(max_age, mean_learning)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = mean_learning - sd, ymax = mean_learning + sd),               alpha = 0.2, fill = "steelblue") +   geom_line(colour = "steelblue", linewidth = 1) +   geom_point(size = 3, colour = "steelblue") +   labs(     title    = "Cephalopod paradox: short lifespans can select for fast learning",     subtitle = paste0("Complex landscape (shrubs); learning_rate free to evolve; ",                       n_reps, " replicates per lifespan, last-50-tick mean"),     x        = "Maximum lifespan (ticks)",     y        = "Evolved learning rate (mean ± SD)"   ) +   theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

Evolution bad science (Smaldino & McElreath 2016). Top: false-positive rate rises publication pressure selects low-effort labs. Bottom: research effort declines parallel. Higher replication rates slow deterioration prevent . Julia session required: run_bad_science() self-contained pure-R simulation.
code: library(clade) library(ggplot2) library(patchwork)  # Compare three replication rates over 500 ticks rep_rates  <- c(0.0, 0.1, 0.5) rep_labels <- c("No replication", "10% replication", "50% replication")  results <- mapply(function(rr, lab) {   df      <- run_bad_science(n_ticks = 500L, replication_rate = rr, seed = 42L)   df$rate <- lab   df }, rep_rates, rep_labels, SIMPLIFY = FALSE)  df <- do.call(rbind, results) df$rate <- factor(df$rate, levels = rev(rep_labels))  p1 <- ggplot(df, aes(t, mean_fpr, colour = rate)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("No replication"  = "#d73027",                                   "10% replication" = "#fc8d59",                                   "50% replication" = "#4575b4"),                        name = NULL) +   labs(title = "False-positive rate rises under publication pressure",        x = "Tick", y = "Mean false-positive rate") +   theme_minimal(base_size = 12)  p2 <- ggplot(df, aes(t, mean_effort, colour = rate)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("No replication"  = "#d73027",                                   "10% replication" = "#fc8d59",                                   "50% replication" = "#4575b4"),                        name = NULL) +   labs(title = "Research effort declines over evolutionary time",        x = "Tick", y = "Mean research effort") +   theme_minimal(base_size = 12)  p1 / p2 + plot_layout(guides = "collect") &   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: grid_rows Integer. Number rows toroidal grid (default 30). grid_cols Integer. Number columns toroidal grid (default 30). n_agents_init Integer. Number agents tick 0 (default 50). max_agents Integer. Hard cap live agents; new offspring rejected exceeded (default 500). max_ticks Integer. Simulation length ticks (default 500).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: energy_init Numeric. Energy agent starts (default 100). energy_max Numeric. Maximum energy agent can hold (default 200). move_cost Numeric. Energy deducted per move action (default 1). idle_cost Numeric. Energy deducted agent move (default 0.5). eat_gain Numeric. Energy gained per unit grass consumed (default 5). min_repro_energy Numeric. Minimum energy required attempt reproduction (default 120). repro_cost Numeric. Energy deducted parent reproduction (default 30). offspring_energy Numeric. Energy given new offspring (default 60). starvation_threshold Numeric. Agent dies energy falls value (default 0).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: brain type controls agents map sensory inputs action decisions. types heritable evolvable. See references entry primary literature. brain_type Character. One "bnn" (default), "ann", "ctrnn", "grn", "transformer", "synthesis", "random".   hidden_layers Integer vector. Hidden layer widths "ann" "bnn" (default c(8L); gives one hidden layer 8 units). Set c(16L, 8L) two hidden layers. input_radius Integer. Radius Moore neighbourhood used building agent's sensory input vector (default 1L). Radius 1 gives standard 8-cell neighbourhood; radius 2 extends 24-cell neighbourhood. Increasing value expands input vector brain's first-layer width accordingly. n_genes Integer. Number genes "grn" brain type (default 20L). Includes sensory input genes action output genes. transformer_history Integer. Number past sensory inputs transformer attends (default 8L). transformer_heads Integer. Number attention heads (default 2L). synthesis_max_rules Integer. Maximum number -rules per agent (default 10L).
code: * `"bnn"` -- **Bayesian Neural Network** (default). Each synaptic weight   is a probability distribution (mean mu, standard deviation sigma)   rather than a fixed value. The genome encodes the prior (mu, sigma);   within lifetime, experience updates the posterior via approximate   Bayesian inference (sigma shrinks as the agent becomes more certain).   Exploration is automatic: high sigma = uncertain = explore. Diploid   genomes connect naturally: heterozygosity at a locus sets the prior   width for that weight. Implemented via Turing.jl.   References: Neal (1996) *Bayesian Learning for Neural Networks*,   Springer; Blundell et al. (2015) Weight Uncertainty in Neural   Networks, *ICML* pp 1613--1622.  * `"ann"` -- **Multilayer Perceptron**. Standard feedforward network with   fixed weights. Architecture set by `hidden_layers`. Compatible with   the `alifeR` ANN format.   Reference: Rumelhart, Hinton & Williams (1986) Learning   representations by back-propagating errors, *Nature* 323:533--536.  * `"ctrnn"` -- **Continuous-Time Recurrent Neural Network**. Each neuron   i has an internal state y_i governed by the ODE:   tau_i * dy_i/dt = -y_i + sum_j(w_ij * sigma(y_j + theta_j)) + I_i.   Produces temporal dynamics, rhythmic behaviour, and autonomous   initiation. Genome encodes tau (time constants), W (weights), and   theta (biases).   Reference: Beer (1995) On the dynamics of small continuous-time   recurrent neural networks, *Adaptive Behavior* 3(4):469--509.  * `"grn"` -- **Gene Regulatory Network**. The genome IS the brain;   no separate neural network. Each locus represents a gene whose   expression level is regulated by other genes. Some genes receive   sensory input; others produce action output. Behaviour emerges from   ~15-30 gene interaction dynamics simulated each tick. Maximally   biologically minimal. When `ploidy = 2`, each regulatory link has   two alleles and methylation directly suppresses gene expression.   References: Kauffman (1993) *The Origins of Order*, Oxford UP;   Watson & Szathmary (2016) How can evolution learn?, *Trends in   Ecology and Evolution* 31(2):147--157.  * `"transformer"` -- **Attention-Based Transformer**. Attends over a   rolling window of the last `transformer_history` sensory inputs.   Genome encodes query/key/value projection weights and feed-forward   layers. Enables agents to integrate temporal context without explicit   memory parameters.   Reference: Vaswani et al. (2017) Attention is all you need,   *NeurIPS* 30.  * `"synthesis"` -- **Formal / Symbolic Rule Synthesis**. The agent's   brain is an evolved symbolic program: a set of IF-THEN rules of the   form (IF condition_1 AND condition_2 THEN action). Rules are encoded   as a structured list. Evolution proceeds by rule mutation   (add/remove/modify a rule) and rule-set crossover (swap rule subsets   between parents). Behaviour is interpretable and human-readable.   Reference: Angeline, Saunders & Pollack (1994) An evolutionary   algorithm that constructs recurrent neural networks, *IEEE   Transactions on Neural Networks* 5(1):54--65; see also Koza (1992)   *Genetic Programming*, MIT Press.  * `"random"` -- Null brain. Chooses actions uniformly at random.   Used as a baseline to confirm that evolved behaviour outperforms   chance.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: Brain energy cost models metabolic cost neural computation, Polyworld simulation (Yaeger 1994). brain_energy_mode Character. One "none", "size", "activity" (default), "prediction_error".   brain_energy_base Numeric. Fixed cost per synaptic weight per tick (default 0.001). brain_energy_activity Numeric. Scaling factor mean absolute activation brain_energy_mode = "activity" (default 0.5).
code: * `"none"` -- no brain energy cost. * `"size"` -- cost proportional to number of synaptic weights. * `"activity"` -- cost = `brain_energy_base * n_weights +   brain_energy_activity * mean(|activations|)`. Larger and more active   brains are more costly. * `"prediction_error"` -- BNN-only; cost proportional to KL divergence   between prior and posterior (measures how much the agent had to   update its beliefs).  Reference: Yaeger (1994) Computational genetics, physiology, metabolism, neural systems, learning, vision, and behavior or PolyWorld: Life in a new context, in *Artificial Life III*, Addison-Wesley, pp 263--298.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: clade supports haploid (default) diploid life cycles. diploid path implements full Mendelian genetics independent assortment recombination. ploidy Integer. 1L = haploid (default); 2L = diploid. diploid case every heritable trait (brain weights, body size, immune strength, cooperation level, etc.) two alleles – one maternal, one paternal. expressed phenotype computed dominance_model birth change within lifetime. n_chromosomes Integer. Number chromosome pairs (default 1L). > 1, chromosomes assort independently meiosis (pair segregates probability 0.5). brain genome split evenly across chromosome pairs. crossover_rate Numeric. Expected number crossover events per chromosome pair per meiosis (Poisson distributed; default 1.0). Set 0 disable recombination. dominance_model Character. maternal paternal alleles combine produce expressed phenotype: * "additive" (default) – phenotype = (maternal + paternal) / 2. * "dominant" – randomly choose one allele locus. * "codominant" – alleles expressed; implemented additive reported separately get_genome_data().
code: Reference: Charlesworth & Charlesworth (2010) *Elements of Evolutionary Genetics*, Roberts & Company, Chapter 5.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: mutation_sd Numeric. Standard deviation Gaussian noise added brain weight reproduction (default 0.1). mutation_rate_evolution Logical. TRUE, mutation_sd heritable diploid trait evolves (default FALSE). Reference: Sniegowski, Gerrish & Lenski (1997) Evolution high mutation rates experimental populations E. coli, Nature 387:703–705. mutation_sd_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean mutation_sd distribution mutation_rate_evolution = TRUE (default 0.1). mutation_sd_min Numeric. Minimum allowed mutation_sd (default 0.001). mutation_sd_max Numeric. Maximum allowed mutation_sd (default 1.0).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: Within-lifetime learning modifies brain without altering genome. learning_rate_evolution = TRUE, learning rate heritable diploid trait; variation learning_rate across population allows Baldwin Effect (Baldwin 1896) observed: genomes encode pre-adapted solutions outcompete relying learning. rl_mode Character. Reinforcement learning update rule: "none" (default), "actor_critic", "hebbian".   learning_rate Numeric. Step size within-lifetime weight updates (default 0.01). learning_rate_evolution Logical. TRUE, learning_rate heritable diploid trait (default FALSE). Enables study Baldwin Effect (Baldwin 1896, Hinton & Nowlan 1987).   learning_rate_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean learning_rate distribution learning_rate_evolution = TRUE (default 0.01). learning_rate_min Numeric. Minimum allowed learning_rate (default 0.0). learning_rate_max Numeric. Maximum allowed learning_rate (default 0.5). plasticity_cost Numeric. Energy cost per unit absolute weight change per tick (default 0.05). Implements metabolic cost synaptic plasticity. Reference: Laughlin, de Ruyter van Steveninck & Anderson (1998) metabolic cost neural information, Nature Neuroscience 1(1):36–41. rl_update_freq Integer. Apply RL update every many ticks (default 1L).
code: * `"actor_critic"` -- REINFORCE with baseline (Williams 1992).   Reward = energy delta per tick. Advantage = reward - running mean   (`value_estimate`). Applied to output weights for the chosen action. * `"hebbian"` -- Hebbian potentiation: weights between co-active   neurons are strengthened proportionally to joint activation   (Hebb 1949).  References: Williams (1992) Simple statistical gradient-following algorithms for connectionist reinforcement learning, *Machine Learning* 8:229--256. Hebb (1949) *The Organisation of Behavior*, Wiley. References: Baldwin (1896) A new factor in evolution, *American Naturalist* 30:441--451. Hinton & Nowlan (1987) How learning can guide evolution, *Complex Systems* 1(3):495--502.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: epigenetics = TRUE, agent carries methylome: Boolean vector length genome. element records whether corresponding locus methylated. Within lifetime, learning events can methylate loci (epigenetic_learning_coupling). reproduction, fraction parent's methylation pattern inherited offspring (epigenetic_inheritance). BNN brains, methylation locus shrinks prior sigma_i (canalization – agent becomes less plastic weight). implements transgenerational epigenetic inheritance (TEI) described Jablonka & Lamb (2005). epigenetics Logical. Enable epigenetic methylation TEI (default FALSE). Reference: Jablonka & Lamb (2005) Evolution Four Dimensions, MIT Press. epigenetic_learning_coupling Numeric [0, 1]. Probability within-lifetime learning event methylates corresponding locus (default 0.10). epigenetic_inheritance Numeric [0, 1]. Fraction parent's methylation marks inherited offspring (default 0.50). remaining marks reset (demethylated) offspring. epigenetic_effect_size Numeric. Factor methylation reduces prior sigma (BNN) mutational variance locus (brain types) (default 0.20). methylation_rate Numeric. Spontaneous methylation probability per locus per tick (default 0.001). demethylation_rate Numeric. Spontaneous demethylation probability per methylated locus per tick (default 0.002).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: n_predators_init Integer. Number predators tick 0 (default 0L). Predators evolving brains ("ann" default) co-evolve prey. predator_energy_init Numeric. Starting energy predators (default 150). predator_attack_cost Numeric. Energy deducted predator per attack attempt (default 2). predator_kill_gain Numeric. Energy predator gains per successful kill (default 50). predator_mutation_sd Numeric. Mutation SD predator brain weights (default 0.1). max_predators Integer. Hard cap predator population (default 20L).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: life_history Character. "iteroparous" (default) "semelparous". Semelparous agents die immediately reproducing. Reference: Stearns (1992) Evolution Life Histories, Oxford . max_age Integer. Maximum lifespan ticks; agents die age regardless energy (default 200L). Set Inf disable. senescence_rate Numeric [0, 1]. Gompertz mortality rate; per-tick death probability scales exp(senescence_rate * age) (default 0 = senescence). Reference: Gompertz (1825) nature function expressive law human mortality, Philosophical Transactions Royal Society 115:513–583. repro_senescence Numeric [0, 1]. Per-tick decline reproduction probability age (default 0 = reproductive senescence). life_history_evolution Logical. TRUE, max_age senescence_rate heritable diploid traits (default FALSE). allee_threshold Integer. Minimum local density reproduction (default 0L = Allee effects ). > 0, agents require least many conspecifics Moore neighbourhood reproduce. Reference: Allee (1931) Animal Aggregations, University Chicago Press.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: body_size_evolution Logical. Enable heritable body size trait (default FALSE). Body size scales metabolic costs (larger = expensive), foraging capacity (larger = grass per tick), energy storage (larger = higher cap). Reference size 1.0 (effect). Reference: Kleiber, M. (1947) Body size metabolic rate, Physiological Reviews 27(4):511–541. body_size_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean body size (default 1.0). body_size_mutation_sd Numeric. Mutation SD body size (default 0.08). body_size_min Numeric. Minimum body size (default 0.3). body_size_max Numeric. Maximum body size (default 3.0).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: metabolic_rate_evolution Logical. Enable heritable metabolic rate (default FALSE). Scales move_cost idle_cost. metabolic_rate_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean metabolic rate (default 1.0). metabolic_rate_mutation_sd Numeric. Mutation SD (default 0.05). metabolic_rate_min Numeric. Minimum (default 0.1). metabolic_rate_max Numeric. Maximum (default 5.0).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: aging_rate_evolution Logical. Enable heritable aging rate (default FALSE). Scales Gompertz senescence exponent. aging_rate_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean aging rate (default 1.0). aging_rate_mutation_sd Numeric. Mutation SD (default 0.05). aging_rate_min Numeric. Minimum (default 0.01). aging_rate_max Numeric. Maximum (default 10.0).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: immune_evolution Logical. Enable heritable immune strength trait (default FALSE). immune_strength_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean (default 0.3). immune_strength_mutation_sd Numeric. Mutation SD (default 0.05). immune_strength_min Numeric. Minimum (default 0.0). immune_strength_max Numeric. Maximum (default 1.0).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: disease Logical. Enable SIR disease dynamics (default FALSE). Reference: Kermack & McKendrick (1927) Contributions mathematical theory epidemics, Proceedings Royal Society 115(772):700–721. disease_seed_prob Numeric [0, 1]. Probability agent initially infected tick 1 (default 0.01). transmission_prob Numeric [0, 1]. Probability transmission susceptible neighbour per infected-susceptible adjacent pair per tick (default 0.1). Effective transmission = transmission_prob * (1 - receiver's immune_strength). disease_duration Integer. Ticks agent remains infectious (default 10L). immune_duration Integer. Ticks immunity recovery (default 20L). , agent returns Susceptible. disease_energy_cost Numeric. Energy deducted per tick infected (default 5). disease_death_prob Numeric [0, 1]. Per-tick probability death infected (default 0.02). Scaled (1 - immune_strength) immune_evolution = TRUE.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: kin_selection Logical. Enable kin altruism (default FALSE). Reference: Hamilton (1964) genetical evolution social behaviour & II, Journal Theoretical Biology 7:1–52. kin_altruism_cost Numeric. Energy transferred donor (default 2.0). Hamilton's rule requires benefit * r > cost. kin_altruism_benefit Numeric. Energy received recipient (default 10.0). kin_altruism_r_min Numeric. Minimum pedigree relatedness r required trigger altruism (default 0.25 = half-siblings). kin_altruism_min_donor_energy Numeric. Donor must least energy donate (default 50).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: cooperation_evolution Logical. Enable heritable cooperation level (default FALSE). Uses spatial public goods game (Nowak & May 1992). Reference: Nowak & May (1992) Evolutionary games spatial chaos, Nature 359:826–829. cooperation_multiplier Numeric. Payoff multiplier M public goods game (default 2.0). cooperator pays cost C; group receives M * sum(contributions) / n_local. cooperation selectively favoured, M must exceed group size. cooperation_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean cooperation level (default 0.5). cooperation_mutation_sd Numeric. Mutation SD (default 0.05). cooperation_cost Numeric. Per-tick energy cost paid cooperator (default 1.0).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: dispersal_evolution Logical. Enable heritable natal dispersal (default FALSE). agent carries dispersal_tendency trait (per-tick probability moving away birthplace). Reduces inbreeding kin competition. Reference: Ronce, O. (2007) feel like rolling stone? Annual Review Ecology, Evolution, Systematics 38:231–253. dispersal_cost Numeric. Energy cost per dispersal step (default 2.0). Agents energy <= 2 x dispersal_cost disperse. dispersal_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean dispersal tendency (default 0.1). dispersal_mutation_sd Numeric. Mutation SD dispersal tendency (default 0.02). dispersal_min Numeric. Minimum dispersal tendency (default 0.0). dispersal_max Numeric. Maximum dispersal tendency (default 0.5).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: parental_care Logical. Enable altricial parental care (default FALSE). Offspring remain parent graduation. care_duration Integer. Ticks offspring remain parental care (default 5L). care_cost_per_offspring Numeric. Energy cost per carried offspring per tick (default 2.0). max_clutch_size Integer. Maximum offspring per reproductive event (default 1L).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: signal_dims Integer. Number signal dimensions (default 0L = ). > 0, agent evolves heritable signal vector preference vector; mates chosen proximity signal preference. signal_cost Numeric. Energy cost per unit signal magnitude per tick (default 0.1). Models honest signalling costs (Zahavi 1975). Reference: Zahavi (1975) Mate selection – selection handicap, Journal Theoretical Biology 53(1):205–214.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: mimicry Logical. Enable heritable toxicity trait predator signal learning (default FALSE). Implements Mullerian Batesian mimicry via Rescorla-Wagner learning predators. Reference: Rescorla & Wagner (1972) theory Pavlovian conditioning, Classical Conditioning II, Appleton-Century-Crofts, pp 64–99.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: niche_construction Logical. Enable shelter building (default FALSE). Shelters reduce predation slow grass regrowth. Reference: Odling-Smee, Laland & Feldman (2003) Niche Construction: Neglected Process Evolution, Princeton . shelter_build_prob Numeric [0, 1]. Probability building shelter unit per tick eligible (default 0.1). shelter_max_depth Integer. Maximum shelter depth per cell (default 5L). shelter_min_energy Numeric. Minimum energy required build (default 80). shelter_decay_prob Numeric [0, 1]. Per-tick probability shelter unit decays (default 0.05).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: scavenging Logical. Enable carrion dynamics (default FALSE). Dead agents deposit body mass carrion; scavengers consume . carrion_fraction Numeric [0, 1]. Fraction energy_init deposited carrion upon agent death (default 0.5). Uses energy_init agent$energy death, body mass change starvation. carrion_decay_rate Numeric [0, 1]. Per-tick fraction carrion decomposes (default 0.1). carrion_eat_gain Numeric. Energy gained per unit carrion consumed (default 3).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: group_defense Logical. Enable dilution-effect predator avoidance (default FALSE). Per-agent predation probability decreases local group size. Reference: Foster & Treherne (1981) Evidence dilution effect selfish herd fish predation marine insect, Nature 293:466–467.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: habitat_preference_evolution Logical. Enable heritable habitat preference (default FALSE). Preference encodes optimal local grass density; agents attracted cells matching preference. Reference: Fretwell & Lucas (1970) territorial behavior factors influencing habitat distribution birds, Acta Biotheoretica 19(1):16–36.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: seasonal_amplitude Numeric. Amplitude sinusoidal grass_rate modulation (default 0 = ). grass_rate_t = grass_rate * (1 + seasonal_amplitude * sin(2 * pi * t / seasonal_period)). seasonal_period Integer. Period seasonal cycle ticks (default 100L).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: speciation Logical. Enable reproductive isolation based genome distance (default FALSE). Agents mate conspecifics (genome distance < isolation_threshold). Reference: Gavrilets (2004) Fitness Landscapes Origin Species, Princeton . isolation_threshold Numeric. Maximum genome distance successful mating (default 0.45). Species inferred hierarchical clustering genome distances logging tick.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: social_learning Logical. Enable copying output-layer weights successful neighbours (default FALSE). Implements prestige bias. Reference: Laland (2004) Social learning strategies, Learning Behavior 32(1):4–14. social_learning_freq Integer. Apply social learning every many ticks (default 10L). social_learning_rate Numeric. Fraction neighbour weights copied (default 0.1).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: world_evolution = TRUE, environment parameters listed world_params_to_evolve treated evolvable quantities change via random drift tick, allowing environment co-evolve agents. extends MAP-Elites search joint agent-environment space. world_evolution Logical. Enable world parameter evolution (default FALSE). world_mutation_sd Numeric. Standard deviation Gaussian perturbations world parameters per tick (default 0.02). world_params_to_evolve Character vector. Names parameters default_specs() treat evolvable. Example: c("grass_rate", "disease_death_prob"). Defaults character(0) (none).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: Adds shrub canopy resource layers top ground grass. Canopy access requires wing_size >= canopy_threshold (heritable). Designed break standard brain-size x longevity correlation. Reference: Liedtke & Fromhage (2019); Isbell (2006). complex_landscape Logical. Enable multi-layer resources (default FALSE). shrub_density Numeric [0, 1]. Initial fraction cells shrub resource (default 0.3). shrub_growth_rate Numeric. Per-tick fractional regrowth shrubs (default 0.03). shrub_energy Numeric. Maximum energy shrub cell provides (default 20.0). canopy_density Numeric [0, 1]. Initial canopy coverage (default 0.15; trees sparse). canopy_growth_rate Numeric. Per-tick fractional regrowth canopy (default 0.005; trees grow slowly). canopy_energy Numeric. Maximum energy canopy cell provides (default 50.0; high energy density aerial specialists). canopy_threshold Numeric. Minimum wing_size needed canopy access (default 0.6). wing_size_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean wing_size (default 0.0; ground-bound founders). wing_size_mutation_sd Numeric. Mutation SD wing_size (default 0.05). wing_size_min Numeric. Minimum wing size (default 0.0). wing_size_max Numeric. Maximum wing size (default 1.0).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: Implements assortative mating invasion range front, causing dispersal-enhancing alleles accumulate frontier without requiring fitness advantage sedentary conspecifics. Reference: Shine et al. (2011) evolutionary process assembles phenotypes space rather time. PNAS 108:5708–5711. Requires dispersal_evolution = TRUE effect. spatial_sorting Logical. Enable range-front mate preference high-dispersal partners (default FALSE). sorting_front_threshold Numeric [0, 1]. Fraction maximum range radius defines "front" (default 0.75; outermost 25%). sorting_mating_boost Numeric. Multiplier applied dispersal_tendency scoring mates front (default 3.0).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: IFfolk = offspring + sum(r x relative's offspring). Agents transfer energy energy-depleted kin. Optional parliament suppression penalises defectors cooperative populations. Reference: Fromhage & Jennions (2019) strategic reference gene. Proc R Soc B 286:20190459. doi:10.1098/rspb.2019.0459 iffolk_selection Logical. Enable IFfolk energy transfers (default FALSE). iffolk_r_min Numeric. Minimum relatedness coefficient relative count (default 0.125 = cousin). iffolk_radius Integer. Spatial search radius relatives grid cells (default 5L). iffolk_transfer Numeric. Maximum energy transferred per altruistic act (default 3.0). iffolk_min_energy Numeric. Minimum donor energy required transfer (default 60.0). parliament_suppression Logical. Enable parliament--genes penalty defectors (negative helper_tendency) surrounded cooperators (default FALSE). parliament_cost Numeric. Energy cost per tick defectors cooperative neighbourhoods (default 0.5).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: log_freq Integer. Log population-level statistics every many ticks (default 1L; set higher reduce memory use long runs). log_genomes Logical. Log flattened genome vectors get_genome_data()$genomes log tick (default FALSE; memory intensive large populations). random_seed Integer NA_integer_. Seed Julia's RNG. NA_integer_ uses random seed (default).

what: Register a custom module function — register_module
title: Register a custom module function — register_module
text: Injects R function clade simulation tick loop specified hook point. function receives simplified environment snapshot (list(agents, grass, t, specs)) must return (modified unmodified). Multiple modules may registered hook point; run registration order.

title: Register a custom module function — register_module
code: register_module(fn, when = "post_tick", name = NULL)

title: Register a custom module function — register_module
text: fn function signature function(env) -> env. Character; one "pre_tick", "post_agents", "post_tick", "post_reproduce". Default "post_tick". name Character label module (optional; used list_modules() output).

title: Register a custom module function — register_module
text: Invisibly NULL. Side-effects: adds module registry.

title: Register a custom module function — register_module
text: Custom modules can modify: env$agents[[]]$energy — agent energy env$agents[[]]$alive — set FALSE kill agent env$grass — resource matrix (numeric matrix, rows x cols)

title: Register a custom module function — register_module
code: # Kill any agent older than 100 ticks register_module(   fn   = function(env) {     for (i in seq_along(env$agents)) {       if (isTRUE(env$agents[[i]]$age > 100)) env$agents[[i]]$alive <- FALSE     }     env   },   when = "post_tick",   name = "max_age_100" ) clear_modules()

title: Run an evolutionary simulation — run_alife
text: run_alife() primary entry point clade. sends specs Julia , runs specs$max_ticks ticks entirely Julia, returns environment list containing final agent population logged statistics. Julia session started automatically first call.

title: Run an evolutionary simulation — run_alife
code: run_alife(specs = default_specs(), verbose = TRUE)

title: Run an evolutionary simulation — run_alife
text: specs named list simulation parameters, typically default_specs() modifications. parameters documented default_specs(). verbose Logical. Print progress messages (default TRUE). Pass FALSE batch runs testing.

title: Run an evolutionary simulation — run_alife
text: env list components: $agents list agent lists, one per surviving agent. $t Final tick number (equals specs$max_ticks). $specs specs list used run (may differ input world_evolution = TRUE). $progress data frame per-tick logged statistics (get_run_data(env)$ticks). $deaths data frame per-death records (get_run_data(env)$deaths). $genome_log list per-tick genome matrices (non-NULL specs$log_genomes = TRUE).

title: Run an evolutionary simulation — run_alife
text: R-Julia boundary crossed per call regardless simulation length agent count. contrasts Rcpp-based simulators (including alifeR) data marshalled across R-C++ boundary every tick.

title: Run an evolutionary simulation — run_alife
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ specs <- default_specs() env   <- run_alife(specs) data  <- get_run_data(env) plot_run(data)  # Diploid run specs$ploidy <- 2L env2 <- run_alife(specs)  # BNN with epigenetics specs$brain_type  <- "bnn" specs$epigenetics <- TRUE env3 <- run_alife(specs) } # }

what: Simulate the evolution of scientific practice — run_bad_science
title: Simulate the evolution of scientific practice — run_bad_science
text: Implements agent-based model Smaldino & McElreath (2016). "Labs" (agent groups) heritable research_power (W; probability given study tests true hypothesis) research_effort (e; investment methodological rigour). false-positive rate per study

title: Simulate the evolution of scientific practice — run_bad_science
code: run_bad_science(   n_labs = 200L,   n_ticks = 500L,   n_studies_per_tick = 5L,   replication_rate = 0,   research_power_init_mean = 0.3,   research_effort_init_mean = 0.8,   mutation_sd = 0.05,   seed = NULL )

title: Simulate the evolution of scientific practice — run_bad_science
text: n_labs Integer; number labs. Default 200. n_ticks Integer; number evolutionary ticks run. Default 500. n_studies_per_tick Integer; studies produced per lab per tick. Default 5. replication_rate Numeric [0, 1]; probability per tick lab attempts replicate one random neighbour's published findings. Replication penalise false positives; tracks failed_reps (attempts fail original false positive). Default 0. research_power_init_mean Numeric; initial mean research power W. Default 0.3. research_effort_init_mean Numeric; initial mean research effort e. Default 0.8. mutation_sd Numeric; standard deviation Gaussian mutation applied traits reproduction. Default 0.05. seed Integer NULL; random seed reproducibility. Default NULL.

title: Simulate the evolution of scientific practice — run_bad_science
text: data frame one row per tick columns: t Tick number. mean_power Mean research_power across labs. mean_effort Mean research_effort across labs. mean_fpr Mean false-positive rate alpha across labs. total_publications Total publications produced tick. failed_replications Number failed replication attempts.

title: Simulate the evolution of scientific practice — run_bad_science
text: alpha = W / (1 + (1 - W) * e) tick, lab produces n_studies_per_tick studies yielding mix true positives (probability W) false positives (probability alpha among studies find true effect). Labs publications reproduce higher rates. Optional replication attempts slow stop deterioration research standards.

title: Simulate the evolution of scientific practice — run_bad_science
text: Smaldino, P.E. & McElreath, R. (2016) natural selection bad science. Royal Society Open Science 3:160384. doi:10.1098/rsos.160384

title: Simulate the evolution of scientific practice — run_bad_science
code: result <- run_bad_science(n_ticks = 200L, seed = 1L) plot(result$t, result$mean_fpr, type = "l",      xlab = "Tick", ylab = "Mean false-positive rate",      main = "Evolution of bad science")
